title: Compounding Systems

content.question: Which compounding action are you breaking too often to see returns?
sop: Pick one compounding action (content, savings, skill practice) you want to build a streak on.
Rate your current Rate 1-10, how much this action is realistically improving per period (see calculation, page 5)
Commit to the minimum viable version daily so the streak survives busy days.
Track your streak length (Time) visibly so a broken chain is obvious immediately (see calculation, page 5)
Review growth quarterly, compounding is invisible week to week and obvious only in hindsight.
questions: Which compounding action are you breaking too often to see returns?
What's one small action you've done consistently for over 90 days without missing?
If you 10x'd the time horizon on your current effort but kept the daily input the same, would the result still feel small?

definitions.def: A feedback loop where success creates more success.

content.equation_example: Base equals 100 (starting units, content pieces, dollars, reps), Rate equals 5% growth per period, Time equals 12 periods. Result equals 100 times 1.05 to the 12th power, approximately 180. Higher/rising means small consistent gains are stacking into a far bigger number than the raw daily effort suggests. Lower/falling means a broken streak is resetting the compounding before it can take off.
content.closing_statement: A broken streak resets more than it looks like.

caption:
Principle Nº 152: Compounding Systems

Small actions repeated → massive outcomes.

Ask yourself: Which compounding action are you breaking too often to see returns?
